review_title: Solve issues and collaborate with team for resolution right there and then
description: Overall experience with OPSGenie is good as it has helped us to effectively manage incidents and ensure resolution from POCs.
author: John Epok P.
pros: - very straightforward incident tracking - immediate alerts sent to commanders and people to ensure collaboration leading to resolution - mobile alerts to ensure people are notified even when they're away from their desktops
cons: - mobile alerts can be annoying sometimes

review_title: Is good for alerts ntofications
description: My experience in two companies, OpsGenie is good, but they can improve to be more than just alerting, to be used for incident managment.
author: Tamer B.
pros: The service was straightforward: send a notification when an alert goes off, and it is easy to create layers of escalation
cons: Reporting was a nightmare, as the list of users and groups wasn't available, and creating incidents was complicated.

review_title: OpsGenie... basic
description: I was on a team which transitioned PagerDuty to OpsGenie and then a few months later back to PagerDuty.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I didn't like too much about OpsGenie other than it was easy to integrate with other Atlassian products.
cons: It was very simplistic and basic compared to PagerDuty, I hope the price reflected that. I would prefer PD over OpsGenie.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Basic incident management platform for startups
description: Satisfied given the amount of money we pay with them
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I like the most about OpsGenie is its notification system. It has built-in SMS notification system, no need to integrate with other platforms & no need to pay extra. It comes with unlimited SMS notification (depending on your plan of course). Also, OpsGenie can notify you via phone call for critical alerts.
cons: One thing OpsGenie is missing is alerts correlation. Basically OpsGenie just receives alerts and notify relative teams according to your notification rule. It would be better if OpsGenie has alerts correlation platform to deduplicate noise alarms

review_title: A very stable tried and true solution to alerts
description: Overall, everything works well and stays working long term. The ability for our team to collaborate during an outage is the most helpful. We could not live without it.
author: Ash B.
pros: The app is very stable and works well on both Android and iOS. The options to integrate are vast and have the ability to connected to almost anything you want to monitor and alerting is amazing
cons: I am concerned about what the migration to Atlassian is going to do for the app and the integrations. Depending on what you're integrating the learning curve is could be high.

review_title: Solid market contender
description: OpsGenie is a great tool for the proactive monitoring of your infrastructure. If your agents spend a decent amount of time in Slack, OpsGenie can notify them about detected issues via notifications. It also allows them to acknowledge alerts and manage things via a simple Slack command. An escalation mechanism that enables making automatic escalation calls to stakeholders allowed us to avoid human errors and to act where the on-call agent did not react. A big plus is its integration with Jira Service Management and the ability to create tickets whenever an issue is detected.
author: Jarosław K.
pros: What I like the most about OpsGenie is its ability to integrate with other tools our company is using, e.g. Slack and Icinga.
cons: Relatively limited options to customize alerts and other aspects of the tool.

review_title: kinda works, but will be gone soon
description: not native to atlassian, is not very well integrated
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: reliable waking up, scheding, multiple ways to notify about the issue
cons: it is being discontinued in a couple of years and we need to migrate

review_title: OpsGenie is a solid on-call solution.
author: Mathieu R.
pros: OpsGenie made on-call coordination smooth. Notifications were fast, escalation rules were easy to adjust, and it integrated well with the systems we already used for monitoring gameplay stability.
cons: The mobile app sometimes felt a bit slower than the web version, though nothing that impacted urgent alerts.

review_title: Effective Platform for Streamlining On-Call and Escalations
description: It’s a reliable and flexible incident management platform that helped improve our response times and on-call coordination.
author: Gary T.
pros: What I liked most about OpsGenie is how it streamlines incident management and on-call coordination.
cons: While the integrations are powerful, some of the configuration steps for custom alert policies weren’t always intuitive and required trial and error.

review_title: Opsgenie review
author: Vishal J.
pros: It integrates seamlessly with various monitoring, ticketing, and collaboration
cons: Opegenie's cost can be high for some small businesses and startups

review_title: Greate & Useful
author: Balazs H.
pros: This is a very useful app where you can manage your Company services like alerts, etc.
cons: I don't had negative experiences with OpsGenie Services

review_title: Reliable Incident Response for Modern IT Teams
author: Manon S.
pros: I get instant notifications when an issue arises, allowing me to resolve it quickly.
cons: The cost can quickly add up if you need more advanced features and integrations.

review_title: OpsGenie Review positives and Negatives.
description: It is very easy to use application. Also highly recommendable if your budget is low.
author: Vibhu S.
pros: It is very easy to use and is very handy. Also the mobile app takes this to another level from where we can access and get notified on time whenever there is an alert that comes in. Also it supports call feature if you failed to acknowledge the alert.
cons: There is no SLA Warning or breached graph on the application that is the only con that I've found on my side.

review_title: OpsGenie review
author: Ayomikun O.
pros: Makes scheduling resource for on calls easy. OpsGenie also comes with sms alerts
cons: Fixing rotations at times gets so complex

review_title: Easy way to Oncall and Incident Management
description: We moved from pagerduty to opsgenie, it was easy to setup and get going. Working out well so far.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - Setting up is simple - Lots of integrations available - Role Based Access Control - One can create good Dashboard - Mobile App is very handy
cons: - Extracting information from the integration and showing it up in the alert is difficult to setup - The user experience could be simplified but that is the case with all atlassian products

review_title: OpsGenie Helped us to handle realtime alerts
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We can identify issues live and take an action immediately
cons: There's no way to change alerts sounds in Android without affecting the operating systems sounds

review_title: I use a daily to notify issues about my apps and works like a charm
description: 1. easier to settings 2. availability (uptime) 3. the message of alert contains great elements
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: 1. is very simple to setting teams, escalations and source notifications 2. reports is easier to use 3. integratetions
cons: 1. the on call schedule isn´t easy in your first visit. 2. integrations with another apps need more docs o information

review_title: Good, almost great
description: Reliable Alerts monitoring from our application and infrastructure. Without it, we'd either be staring at monitors all night, or waiting for customers to call us and complain. Critical critical app for us.
author: William N.
pros: The scheduling, routing, and alerting of the alerts is spectacular. Really simple to use, really powerful.
cons: The new ui for alerts. Yes, its more flexible and exposes more functionalilty that was previously possible. But for 99% of the time its too, too much UI flexibility. When the house is on fire, I want to grab the fire estinguisher and go. Your ui slows me down. It just needs to let me select open, unacked, and closed alerts, SUPER SUPER easily and SUPER SUPER FAST. Also, the Android app is often unusably slow. I can often boot up my laptop, login to our domain, unlock password manager, and login into the opsgenine website before the stupid app will actually display the alert. Terrible.

review_title: Very useful paging tool
description: Overall, I have had no problems with OpsGenie. It has been a great tool to support my on-call job duties and I'll very much recommend it.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Mobile application makes paging very easy and a great UI provided by the developers makes it easier to navigate even when using the mobile application
cons: There have often been cases where the incident got escalated even when the alert was acknowledged. I'd say there was a miss about the defined SLA.

review_title: Persistent NOC Operator
description: It's an excellent software. It does the job that it's supossed to, and keeps logs of all its actions.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: * Sometimes NOC operators hesitate to reach out to Sysadmins when an alert comes in the middle to the night, thus losing valuable time when an unidentified outage appears. OpsGenie does not hesitate, and if proper configured, it'll have a sysadmin looking into that alert faster than a NOC operator could.
cons: * Only sometimes, even when an alert has been closed, the software keeps calling you. I think software engineers have to work better with queues.

review_title: Strong offering as part of Jira
description: It does what we need it to do and integrates well with many different systems we use. I am satisfied with the product.
author: Jonathan M.
pros: OpsGenie was not the primary driver for us to select Jira for service management, but Ops Genie does a fine job of integrating with our platforms to alert users to failures. it is reliable.
cons: We have an international staff and the SMS features are not available in many of the countries in which we work.

review_title: Super product!
description: Support has been good. The chat feature is nice.
author: Chad B.
pros: The ability to write complex rulesets and escalations.
cons: Needs some more options around scheduling on-call rotations and notification policies for how we use it. Android app can also use some refinement.

review_title: Opsgenie use at Dunzo
description: For daily usecases opsgenie works very smoothly. Anyplace where customisation is needed, it is not easy to work with
author: Shitij G.
pros: Easy to setup basic alerting and routing. On call management is also flexible. Has got lots of integrations
cons: Most good integrations are only within atlassian ecosystem. Anything over basic use is complex and UX is not very intuitive. Incident management plan is not cheap

review_title: We looked at the rest, we took OpsGenie!
author: Matt J.
pros: OpsGenie provides a service similar to the other On Call distribution tools, but we decided on OpsGenie due to their competitive cost, and a sense that they would respond to our feature requests, and our many demands! We wanted all of our developers to be on call, not just our ops team, so we quickly found other solutions were priced out of our range. OpsGenie price was fair, and allowed us to spread responsibility to all of the people in our organization that deserve a 2AM wake up call. Also, they have proven to be responsive to our requests.
cons: The UI has so many options, that it can be a little hard to find exactly what you need if you aren't familiar with it. But it doesn't take long to figure our where things are.

review_title: Overall positive, it does what it is supposed to.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Good possibilities in terms of choosing notification medium and also the ability to set yearly settings is a big plus.
cons: the app could be improved as it is quite minimal and also more admin rights to be accessible would improve the whole experience.
